entries.title: BMW’s AI Runs 16 Billion Daily Requests and 600 Use Cases
entries.summary: Every time a BMW driver locks their car remotely, checks their battery level, receives a navigation update or gets a software fix pushed over the air, that counts as a request. With 24.5 million connected vehicles, those small interactions add up fast. entries.summary: OpenAI said Friday (June 26) that it is limiting the release of its new GPT-5.6 series artificial intelligence (AI) models to a small group of trusted partners at the request of the U.S. government. entries.title: AI-Generated Fake Receipts Now Make Up 71% of Expense Fraud
entries.summary: Four in 10 U.S. employees have used artificial intelligence to generate a fake expense receipt, according to a survey released Tuesday (June 23) by expense management platform Emburse. entries.title: Kalshi Hits $5.5B in Crypto Perps, Expanding Beyond Prediction Markets
entries.summary: Kalshi’s crypto perpetual futures generated more than $5.5 billion in trading volume in their first two weeks. entries.summary: Australia's corporate watchdog is taking HSBC to the Federal Court over a prolonged failure to protect customers from scammers, in what could become a defining case for how banks globally are held accountable for scam-related losses. entries.title: Revolut ends remote-first working for graduates and interns
entries.summary: Revolut is scrapping its remote-first policy for graduates and interns, requiring new staffers to work in the office at least three days a week.

entries.title: Binance to stop serving EU customers after failing to secure MiCA license
entries.summary: Crypto giant Binance has told customers in the EU that it will stop providing them with services from 1 July after it failed to secure a license under the bloc’s Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation.
